data.narrative:
Vannevar Labs, a Series B defense and security technology company, carries a current post-money valuation of $1.5B following $90M in total capital raised. The peer universe comprises 10 comparable firms: 2 public companies (DRSHF, IDEAFORGE.NS) and 8 private enterprises operating in the same sector and stage. The primary private comparables—Dream Security ($1.5B), Harmattan AI ($1.4B), Code Metal ($1.2B), Castelion ($1.0B), and Defense Unicorns ($1.0B)—establish a median valuation of $1.0B across the 7-company peer set, with a range spanning $300M to $1.5B.

Two triangulation methods apply to the valuation. Stage-progression analysis, applying a 1.9x multiplier typical of Series B-to-next-round advancement, yields $2.9B. Peer-median comparison anchors to the $1.0B median across same-sector, same-stage companies. These methods produce a wide divergence, reflecting uncertainty around growth trajectory and relative competitive positioning within the cohort.

The triangulated analysis supports a valuation range of $850M (low) to $3.3B (high), with a median of $1.9B. The current $1.5B valuation sits within this range, slightly below the median estimate. Key uncertainties include stage-progression timing and the appropriate peer weighting—Vannevar's observed clustering near the upper quartile of the peer set suggests either above-median growth expectations or differentiated positioning, but this premium relative to the median peer ($1.0B) remains unresolved without additional operational or market share data.
